commit.message:
FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts to fix scene_texture crash (#380)

* Initial plan

* FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts for proper GL isolation

Fixes _mesa_ReadPixels crash in SoSceneTexture2P::updatePBuffer when the
Rendering/scene_texture demo is shown in the obol_viewer System GL panel.

Root cause: SoOffscreenRenderer sets SORTED_OBJECT_BLEND transparency which
forces updatePBuffer (not updateFrameBuffer). updatePBuffer needs real GL
context isolation — it assumes makeContextCurrent switches to a fresh inner
context so that glReadPixels sees only tmpFbo, not the outer renderer's FBO.
The old FLTKContextManager shared one GL context for everything, so the outer
FBO binding leaked into the inner "context", causing the Mesa crash.

Fix (X11/GLX): ensureWindow() now records mainGLCtx_ and mainDrawable_ after
the first make_current(). createOffscreenContext() creates a real GLXContext
via glXCreateContext(dpy, vi, mainGLCtx_, True) — sharing textures/VBOs with
the FLTK context but having isolated GL state. makeContextCurrent() uses
glXMakeCurrent() to actually switch; restorePreviousContext() switches back.
Windows/macOS fall back to the original single-context approach (unchanged).

Co-authored-by: starseeker <238416+starseeker@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: expose clipboard API on Window (SetClipboard/GetClipboard)

Wire internal/x11/selection and internal/wayland/datadevice up to the
public Window type via two new methods:

  func (w *Window) SetClipboard(text string) error
  func (w *Window) GetClipboard() (string, error)

Implementation details:
- clipboard.go: SetClipboard/GetClipboard dispatch to Wayland or X11
  backend based on app.displayServer.  A serveClipboardSource goroutine
  services compositor Receive requests until the source is cancelled.
- x11SelectionAdapter wraps *x11client.Connection to satisfy
  selection.Conn (the two SendRequest signatures differ; adapter
  translates opcode+data to a full X11 wire buffer).  GetProperty and
  DeleteProperty are implemented directly on the adapter.
- App gains x11SelectionMgr, waylandDataDeviceMgr, and waylandDataDevice
  fields, initialised in initX11Clipboard and setupWaylandDataDevice
  helpers to keep initX11Window and bindInputDevices below CC 10.
- bindWaylandGlobals binds wl_data_device_manager (optional/non-fatal).
- selectClipboardMime extracts MIME negotiation from getWaylandClipboard,
  keeping all new functions below CC 10.

Tests: clipboard_test.go covers NoDisplay, nil-manager, and mock-backed
paths for both backends plus goroutine cancellation.

Fixes: AUDIT.md 'Clipboard not exposed in public API'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: truncateForDiscord exceeds 2000-char limit; increase sendAndWait timeout to 10m

Two bugs found in systemd logs after MCP usage:

1. truncateForDiscord was slicing to 1990 then appending a 24-char suffix,
   producing strings of 2014 chars — Discord rejects anything over 2000.
   Fix: compute suffix length explicitly and slice to (2000 - suffix.length).

2. sendAndWait had a hardcoded 5-minute timeout. Complex MCP tasks (e.g. n8n
   workflow analysis) took ~4 minutes, hitting the timeout and returning an
   error to Discord while Copilot had already completed the work.
   Fix: default to 10 minutes, configurable via COPILOT_TIMEOUT_MS env var.

Also documents MCP_INPUT_* and COPILOT_TIMEOUT_MS in .env.example.

feat: MCP server integration — load CLI config, per-session workspace + toggles

Automatically loads MCP servers from the user's existing CLI config files:
- ~/.config/Code/User/mcp.json  (global; "mcpServers" key)
- <workingDir>/.vscode/mcp.json (workspace; "servers" key)

Both files are merged (workspace wins on name conflict) and passed to
createSession/resumeSession via the SDK's mcpServers config option.

Key details:
- McpConfigLoader resolves ${input:xxx} from env MCP_INPUT_XXX; drops
  servers with unresolved inputs rather than crashing (logs warning)
- Input ID regex restricted to [\w-] to avoid invalid env var names
- Per-session workingDirOverrides + mcpToolOverrides Maps on SessionManager
- buildMcpConfig(key) merges base config + session tool-filter overrides
- getMcpStatus correctly shows skipped servers as skipped even when overridden

New /chat and /ask option:
  workspace: optional path — sets working dir before session creation so
  the right .vscode/mcp.json is loaded without needing /reset afterward

New /mcp slash command:
  list     — show all configured servers (source, enabled/skipped status)
  enable   — set tools to ["*"] for a server
  disable  — set tools to [] for a server
  workspace — set working directory (affects MCP loading on next /reset)

commit.message:
Implement X11 window management: SetTitle, SetMinSize, SetMaxSize, SetFullscreen

Add InternAtom, ChangeProperty, and SendEvent methods to the X11 client
Connection type, then wire them into the four window-management methods
that were previously silent no-ops on the X11 backend.

SetTitle now writes both WM_NAME (STRING) and _NET_WM_NAME (UTF8_STRING)
so that legacy and modern window managers show the correct title.

SetMinSize and SetMaxSize write WM_NORMAL_HINTS using the 72-byte
ICCCM WM_SIZE_HINTS structure with the appropriate PMinSize/PMaxSize flags.

SetFullscreen sends a _NET_WM_STATE ClientMessage to the root window per
the EWMH specification, adding or removing _NET_WM_STATE_FULLSCREEN.

Adds predefined X11 atom IDs (AtomString, AtomWMName, AtomWMNormalHints,
AtomWMSizeHints) and new opcodes (OpcodeInternAtom, OpcodeSendEvent) to
the wire package.

Fixes AUDIT.md HIGH finding: X11 window-management operations are silent no-ops.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
